question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a ContractRoutes endpoint and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath is an async function that resolves before the route handler sets responseDataLabel, causing the responseHandler to read an undefined responseDataLabel and throw a NotFoundError, effectively denying access to fee data for legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/tokens and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath constructs requestPathLabel from req.baseUrl + req.route.path (both attacker-influenced via routing), causing the responseHandler to select a per-path cache-control header that sets a very long max-age for responses containing token fee relationship data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a BlockRoutes endpoint (/blocks/) with a trailing slash, causing recordRequestPath's .replace(/\\/+$/g, '') to strip the slash and produce /api/v1/blocks as requestPathLabel, which then matches a specific headers.path[] entry with a longer cache TTL than /api/v1/blocks/, causing block fee data to be cached longer than intended?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash where transactionIdOrHash is a valid Ethereum transaction hash, and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath sets requestPathLabel to /api/v1/contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash, which may not match any headers.path[] entry, causing responseHandler to apply default cache headers to responses containing gas_price and charged_tx_fee, potentially over-caching fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash/actions and exploit the route ordering in contractRoute.js (where /results/:transactionIdOrHash is registered after /results/logs) to cause Express to match the wrong route, resulting in recordRequestPath setting an incorrect requestPathLabel that maps to wrong cache headers for fee-containing action responses?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/:contractId/results/logs (which has a manual res.locals[requestPathLabel] assignment in getContractLogsById) and then immediately send a request to /contracts/:contractId/results/:consensusTimestamp, exploiting a shared res.locals state (if any middleware reuses objects) to cause recordRequestPath to skip its assignment and apply wrong cache headers to fee data in the second response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to an AccountRoutes endpoint with a Unicode-encoded idOrAliasOrEvmAddress that, when concatenated in req.baseUrl + req.route.path, produces a requestPathLabel containing Unicode path separators, causing the headers.path[] lookup to fail and responseHandler to apply default cache headers to responses containing max_custom_fees data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath sets requestPathLabel to /api/v1/accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards, which may match a headers.path[] entry with a specific cache-control header, causing staking reward fee data to be cached with an unintended TTL and served stale to other users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a ContractRoutes endpoint and exploit the fact that the extendExpress wrap() function calls next() automatically after the async recordRequestPath resolves, so that if recordRequestPath sets requestPathLabel to a value that causes responseHandler to select a cache-control header with max-age=0, fee data is never cached and every request hits the database, causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /blocks/:hashOrNumber where hashOrNumber is the string 'results', causing Express to potentially match the /contracts/results route instead of /blocks/:hashOrNumber, resulting in recordRequestPath setting an incorrect requestPathLabel that maps to wrong cache headers for fee data in the response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/:contractId/state and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath sets requestPathLabel without validating that the path corresponds to a fee-bearing endpoint, causing the responseHandler to apply fee-endpoint-specific cache headers to state responses, potentially leaking fee-related cache configuration details?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to an AccountRoutes endpoint with a null byte (%00) in the idOrAliasOrEvmAddress parameter, causing req.baseUrl + req.route.path in recordRequestPath to produce a requestPathLabel containing a null byte, which then fails the headers.path[] lookup and causes responseHandler to apply default cache headers to responses containing max_custom_fees data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/:contractId/results and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath sets requestPathLabel to the route pattern (/:contractId/results) rather than the resolved path, causing the metricsHandler's toOpenApiPath() to convert it to /{contractId}/results, which may not match the expected OpenAPI path and cause fee-related metrics to be misattributed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a ContractRoutes endpoint with a contractId that is a valid EVM address (0x-prefixed 20-byte hex), causing recordRequestPath to set requestPathLabel to a path containing the literal EVM address rather than the route pattern, which then fails the headers.path[] lookup and causes responseHandler to apply default cache headers to responses containing gas_price fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath sets requestPathLabel using req.route.path (the pattern /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts) rather than the full path, causing the responseHandler to look up headers.path['/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts'] instead of the full path, potentially applying wrong cache headers to responses containing NFT transfer fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a BlockRoutes endpoint and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath is applied via router.useExt() (after route handlers) rather than before them, causing the requestPathLabel to be set after the route handler has already populated responseDataLabel with fee data, and then be overwritten by a subsequent middleware that also sets requestPathLabel?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/results/logs and exploit the fact that the contractController manually sets res.locals[requestPathLabel] in getContractLogs before recordRequestPath runs, causing the !res.locals[requestPathLabel] guard to prevent recordRequestPath from overwriting it, and then craft a second request where the manually-set path maps to a cache-control header with a longer TTL for fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/:contractId/results/:consensusTimestamp where the getContractResultsByTimestamp handler checks if requestPathLabel is already in res.locals and returns early if so, and exploit this early-return behavior to cause recordRequestPath to set requestPathLabel to a value that maps to wrong cache headers for responses containing gas_price and max_fee_per_gas?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/crypto with a spender.id query parameter that causes the response to include allowance fee data, and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath does not include query parameters in requestPathLabel, causing the cache to serve the same cached response (with potentially different fee amounts) to requests with different spender.id values?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a ContractRoutes endpoint and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath uses req.route?.path (optional chaining) which returns undefined if req.route is null, causing the path variable to be undefined, the if condition to be false, and requestPathLabel to remain unset, so that responseHandler uses req.route.path directly and throws a TypeError if req.route is also null at that point?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /blocks/:hashOrNumber with a hashOrNumber value that is a valid block hash (0x-prefixed 32-byte hex), causing recordRequestPath to set requestPathLabel to /api/v1/blocks/:hashOrNumber, and then exploit the fact that this path may have a different cache-control TTL than /api/v1/blocks/:hashOrNumber for block number lookups, causing fee data from block hash lookups to be cached differently than block number lookups?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/:contractId/results/logs and exploit the route conflict resolution comment ('must add after /:contractId/results/logs for proper conflict resolution') to cause Express to match /contracts/:contractId/results/:consensusTimestamp instead, resulting in recordRequestPath setting requestPathLabel to the wrong route pattern and applying incorrect cache headers to responses containing gas_price fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to an AccountRoutes endpoint and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath is registered as the last middleware via router.useExt() (after all route handlers), causing it to run after the route handler has set responseDataLabel, and then manipulate req.baseUrl to produce a requestPathLabel that causes responseHandler to apply a cache-control header with a very long max-age to responses containing max_custom_fees data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/:contractId/results/:consensusTimestamp with hbar=false and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath sets requestPathLabel without the hbar parameter, causing the response cache to store the weibar-denominated response under the same path-based cache key as the tinybar response, and then serve the weibar gas_price value to a client that did not specify hbar=false?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/tokens and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath sets requestPathLabel to /api/v1/accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/tokens, which may match a headers.path[] entry with a specific cache-control header, causing token fee relationship data (including custom fee schedules) to be cached with an unintended TTL?
